verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “These words spake Jesus, and lifted up His eyes to heaven, and saith, Father, the hour is come; glorify Thy Son, that Thy Son also may glorify Thee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He that hath done and taught,” it saith, “the same shall be called great in the Kingdom of heaven.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And with much reason; for to show true wisdom in words, is easy, but the proof which is by works is the part of some noble and great one.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore also Christ, speaking of the endurance of evil, putteth Himself forth, bidding us take example from Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: On this account too, after this admonition, He betaketh Himself to prayer, teaching us in our temptations to leave all things, and flee to God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For because He had said, “In the world ye shall have tribulation,” and had shaken their souls, by the prayer He raiseth them again.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As yet they gave heed unto Him as to a man; and for their sake He acteth thus, just as He did in the case of Lazarus, and there telleth the reason; “Because of the people that stand by I said it, that they might believe that Thou hast sent Me.” ( c. xi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Yea,” saith some one, “this took place with good cause in the case of the Jews; but wherefore in that of the disciples?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: With good cause in the case of the disciples also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For they who, after all that had been said and done, said, “Now we know that Thou knowest” ( c. xvi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 30 ), most of all needed to be established.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Besides, the Evangelist doth not even call the action prayer; but what saith he?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He lifted up His eyes to heaven,” and saith rather that it was a discoursing with the Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And if elsewhere he speaks of prayer, and at one time shows Him kneeling on His knees, at another lifting His eyes to heaven, be not thou troubled; for by these means we are taught the earnestness which should be in our petitions, that standing we should look up, not with the eyes of the flesh only, but of the mind, and that we should bend our knees, bruising our own hearts.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For Christ came not merely to manifest Himself, but also about to teach virtue ineffable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But it behooveth the teacher to teach, not by words only, but also by actions.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us hear then what He saith in this place.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Father, the hour is come; glorify Thy Son, that Thy Son also may glorify Thee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Again He showeth us, that not unwilling He cometh to the Cross.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For how could He be unwilling, who prayed that this might come to pass, and called the action “glory,” not only for Himself the Crucified, but also for the Father? since this was the case, for not the Son only, but the Father also was glorified.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For before the Crucifixion, not even the Jews knew Him; “Israel,” it saith, “hath not known Me” ( Isa. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 3 ); but after the Crucifixion, all the world ran to Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then He speaketh also of the manner of the glory, and how He will glorify Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “As Thou hast given Him power over all flesh,” “that nothing which Thou hast given Him should perish.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For to be always doing good, is glory to God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But what is, “As Thou hast given Him power over all flesh”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He now showeth, that what belongs to the preaching is not confined to the Jews alone, but is extended to all the world, and layeth down beforehand the first invitations to the Gentiles.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And since He had said, “Go not into the way of the Gentiles” ( Matt. x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 5 ), and after this time is about to say, “Go ye, and make disciples of all nations” ( Matt. xxviii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 19 ), He showeth that the Father also willeth this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For this greatly offended the Jews, and the disciples too; nor indeed after this did they easily endure to lay hold on the Gentiles, until they received the teaching of the Spirit; because hence arose no small stumblingblock for the Jews.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, when Peter after such a manifestation of the Spirit came to Jerusalem, he could scarcely, by relating the vision of the sheet, escape the charges brought against him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But what is, “Thou hast given Him power over all flesh”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: I will ask the heretics, “When did He receive this power? was it before He formed them, or after?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He himself saith, that it was after that He had been crucified, and had risen again; at least then He said, “All power is given unto Me” ( Matt. xxviii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 18 ), and, “Go ye and make disciples of all nations.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then, had He not authority over His own works?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Did He make them, and had He not authority over them after having made them?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet He is seen doing all in times of old, punishing some as sinners, (for, “Surely I will not hide,” it saith, “from My servant Abraham, that which I am about to do”— Gen. xviii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 17 , LXX.,) and honoring others as righteous.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Had He then the power at that time, and now had He lost it, and did He again receive it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What devil could assert this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But if His power was the same both then and now, (for, saith He, “as the Father raiseth up the dead and quickeneth them, even so the Son quickeneth whom He will”— c. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 21 ,) what is the meaning of the words?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He was about to send them to the Gentiles; in order therefore that they might not think that this was an innovation, because He had said, “I am not sent, save unto the lost sheep of the house of Israel” ( Matt. xv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 24 ), He showeth that this seemeth good to the Father also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And if He saith this with great meanness of circumstance, it is not wonderful.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For so He edified both those at that time, and those who came afterwards; and as I have before said, He always by the excess of meanness firmly persuaded them that the words were those of condescension.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But what is, “Of all flesh”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For certainly not all believed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet, for His part, all believed; and if men gave no heed to His words, the fault was not in the teacher, but in those who received them not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “That He should give eternal life to as many as Thou hast given Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If here also He speaketh in a more human manner, wonder not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For He doth so both on account of the reasons I have given, and to avoid the saying anything great concerning Himself; since this was a stumblingblock to the hearers because as yet they imagined nothing great concerning Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: John, for example, when He speaks in his own person, doth not so, but leadeth up his language to greater sublimity, saying, “All things were made by Him, and without Him was not anything made” ( c. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 3, 4, 9, 11 ); and that He was “Life”; and that He was “Light”; and that “He came to His own”: he saith not, that He would not have had power, had He not received it, but that He gave to others also “power to become sons of God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And Paul in like manner calleth Him equal with God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But He Himself asketh in a more human way, saying thus, “That He should give eternal life to as many as Thou hast given Him.” ( Philip. ii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And this is life eternal, that they might know Thee the only true God, and Jesus Christ whom Thou hast sent.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “The only true God,” He saith, by way of distinction from those which are not gods; for He was about to send them to the Gentiles.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But if they will not allow this, but on account of this word “only” reject the Son from being true God, in this way as they proceed they reject Him from being God at all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For He also saith, “Ye seek not the glory which is from the only God.” ( c. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Well then; shall not the Son be God?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But if the Son be God, and the Son of the Father who is called the Only God, it is clear that He also is true, and the Son of Him who is called the Only true God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why, when Paul saith, “Or I only and Barnabas” ( 1 Cor. ix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 6 ), doth he exclude Barnabas?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Not at all; for the “only” is put by way of distinction from others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And, if He be not true God, how is He “Truth”? for truth far surpasses what is true.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What shall we call the not being a “true” man, tell me? shall we not call it the not being a man at all? so if the Son is not true God, how is He God?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And how maketh He us gods and sons, if He is not true?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But on these matters we have spoken more particularly in another place; wherefore let us apply ourselves to what follows.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I have glorified Thee on the earth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Well said He, “on the earth”; for in heaven He had been already glorified, having His own natural glory, and being worshiped by the Angels.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Christ then speaketh not of that glory which is bound up with His Essence, (for that glory, though none glorify Him, He ever possesseth in its fullness,) but of that which cometh from the service of men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And so the, “Glorify Me,” is of this kind; and that thou mayest understand that He speaketh of this manner of glory, hear what follows.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I have finished the work which Thou gavest Me that I should do it.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And yet the action was still but beginning, or rather was not yet beginning.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How then said He, “I have finished”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Either He meaneth, that “I have done all My part”; or He speaketh of the future, as having already come to pass; or, which one may say most of all, that all was already effected, because the root of blessings had been laid, which fruits would certainly and necessarily follow, and from His being present at and assisting in those things which should take place after these.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: On this account He saith again in a condescending way, “Which Thou gavest Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For had He indeed waited to hear and learn, this would have fallen far short of His glory.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For that He came to this of His own will, is clear from many passages.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As when Paul saith, that “He so loved us, as to give Himself for us” ( Eph. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 2 ); and, “He emptied Himself, and took upon Him the form of a servant” ( Philip. ii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 7 ); and, “As the Father hath loved Me, so have I loved you.” ( c. xv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And now, O Father, glorify Thou Me with Thine Own Self, with the glory which I had with Thee before the world was.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Where is that glory?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For allowing that He was with reason unhonored among men, because of the covering which was put around Him; how seeketh He to be glorified with the Father?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then saith He here?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The saying refers to the Dispensation; since His fleshly nature had not yet been glorified, not having as yet enjoyed incorruption, nor shared the kingly throne.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore He said not “on earth,” but “with Thee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This glory we also shall enjoy according to our measure, if we be sober.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore Paul saith, “If so be that we suffer with Him, that we may also be glorified together.” ( Rom. viii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Ten thousand tears then do they merit, who through sluggishness and sleep plot against themselves when such glory is set before them; and, were there no hell, they would be more wretched than any, who, when it is in their power to reign and to be glorified with the Son of God, deprive themselves of so great blessings.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since if it were necessary to be cut in pieces, if to die ten thousand deaths, if to give up every day ten thousand lives and as many bodies, ought we not to submit to such things for such glory?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But now we do not even despise money, which hereafter, though unwilling, we shall leave: we do not despise money, which brings about us ten thousand mischiefs, which remains here, which is not our own.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For we are but stewards of that which is not our own, although we receive it from our fathers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But when there is hell besides, and the worm that dieth not, and the fire that is not quenched, and the gnashing of teeth, how, tell me, shall we bear these things?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How long will we refuse to see clearly, and spend our all on daily fightings, and contentions, and unprofitable talk, feeding, cultivating earth, fattening the body and neglecting the soul, making no account of necessary things, but much care about things superfluous and unprofitable?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And we build splendid tombs, and buy costly houses, and draw about with us herds of all kinds of servants, and devise different stewards, appointing managers of lands, of houses, of money, and managers of those managers; but as to our desolate soul, we care nothing for that.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And what will be the limit to this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Is it not one belly that we fill, is it not one body that we clothe?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What is this great bustle of business?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why and wherefore do we cut up and tear to pieces the one soul, which we have had assigned to us, in attending to the service of such things, contriving for ourselves a grievous slavery?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For he who needs many things is the slave of many things, although he seem to be their master.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since the lord is the slave even of his domestics, and brings in another and a heavier mode of service; and in another way also he is their slave, not daring without them to enter the agora, nor the bath, nor the field, but they frequently go about in all directions without him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He who seems to be master, dares not, if his slaves be not present, to go forth from home, and if whilst unattended he do but put his head out of his house, he thinks that he is laughed at.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Perhaps some laugh at us when we say this, yet on this very account they would be deserving of ten thousand tears.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For to show that this is slavery, I would gladly ask you, wouldest thou wish to need some one to put the morsel to thy mouth, and to apply the cup to thy lips?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wouldest thou not deem such a service worthy of tears?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What if thou didst require continually supporters to enable thee to walk, wouldest thou not think thyself pitiable, and in this respect more wretched than any?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So then thou oughtest to be disposed now.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For it matters nothing whether one is so treated by irrational things, or by men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why, tell me, do not the Angels differ from us in this respect, that they do not want so many things as we do?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore the less we need, the more we are on our way to them; the more we need, the more we sink down to this perishable life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And that thou mayest learn that these things are so, ask those who have grown old which life they deem happiest, that when they were helplessly mastered, or now when they are masters of these things?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: We have mentioned these persons, because those who are intoxicated with youth, do not even know the excess of their slavery.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For what of those in fever, do they call themselves happy when, thirsting much, they drink much and need more, or when, having recovered their health, they are free from the desire?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou that in every instance the needing much is pitiable, and far apart from true wisdom, and an aggravation of slavery and desire?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why then do we voluntarily increase to ourselves wretchedness?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For, tell me, if it were possible to live uninjured without roof or walls, wouldest thou not prefer this; wherefore then dost thou increase the signs of thy weakness?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Do we not for this call Adam happy, that he needed nothing, no house, no clothes?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Yes,” saith some one, “but now we are in need of them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why then do we make our need greater?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If many persons curtail many of the things actually needed, (servants, I mean, and houses, and money,) what excuse can we have if we overstep the need?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The more thou puttest about thee, the more slavish dost thou become; for by whatever proportion thou requirest more, in that proportion thou hast trenched upon thy freedom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For absolute freedom is, to want nothing at all; the next is, to want little; and this the Angels and their imitators especially possess.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But for men to succeed in this while tarrying in a mortal body, think how great praise this hath.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This also Paul said, when writing to the Corinthians, “But I spare you,” and, “lest such should have trouble in the flesh.” ( 1 Cor. vii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Riches are called “usables,” that we may “use” them rightly, and not keep and bury them; for this is not to possess them, but to be possessed by them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since if we are going to make this our aim how to multiply them, not that we may employ them rightly, the order is reversed, and they possess us, not we them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then free ourselves from this grievous bondage, and at last become free.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why do we devise ten thousand different chains for ourselves?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Is not the bond of nature enough for thee, and the necessity of life, and the crowd of ten thousand affairs, but dost thou twine also other nets for thyself, and put them about thy feet?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And when wilt thou lay hold on heaven, and be able to stand on that height?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For a great thing, a great thing is it, that even having cut asunder all these cords, thou shouldest be able to lay hold on the city which is above.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So many other hindrances are there; all which that we may conquer, let us keep to the mean estate [and having put away superfluities, let us keep to what is necessary.]

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thus shall we lay hold on eternal life, through the grace and lovingkindness of our Lord Jesus Christ, to whom be glory for ever and ever.
